Bollywood's culture of stardom is historically individual and competitive, and male stars publicly taking a back seat to celebrate their partners' professional milestones is not a norm that the industry has been known for. Ranbir and Alia, who married in 2022, have been careful about their public appearances together, which made this moment feel more organic than staged.

For Alia, heading into what promises to be a high-profile release, the wave of positive sentiment generated by the viral clip is a welcome boost — one that money cannot buy and no PR firm could have scripted quite so effectively.
key_facts: Ranbir Kapoor publicly supported Alia Bhatt's upcoming film Alpha
Video of the gesture went viral online
Ranbir and Alia Bhatt married in 2022

stories.headline: South OTT and theatrical releases this week: Blast, Lingam and Ananthan Kaadu lead the lineup (June 22-28, 2026)
stories.what: A strong slate of South Indian films is hitting OTT platforms and theatres this week, with Blast, Lingam, and Ananthan Kaadu leading the charge for the June 22-28, 2026 window. The week promises diverse offerings across Tamil, Telugu, and Malayalam audiences, spanning theatrical outings and straight-to-streaming premieres. It is shaping up as one of the fuller release weeks for South Indian content in recent memory.
stories.lens: With multiple South Indian titles releasing simultaneously on OTT and in cinemas, fans across language communities have a genuinely packed week of new content to choose from.
stories.article:
South Indian cinema is delivering a packed week of new content, with Blast, Lingam, and Ananthan Kaadu headlining a busy schedule of OTT and theatrical releases between June 22 and 28, 2026. The lineup, highlighted by Outlook India, spans multiple languages and formats, underlining the continuing momentum of South Indian storytelling both on streaming platforms and in multiplexes.

Blast and Lingam are among the titles generating the most anticipation heading into the week, while Ananthan Kaadu adds to what appears to be a strong showing for Malayalam or Tamil-language content. The week's spread across both theatrical and OTT windows reflects the evolving distribution strategies of South Indian studios, many of which now green-light projects with a dual-window release model in mind from the very beginning.

For streaming audiences, a Moneycontrol report noted at least nine South OTT releases available to watch this week, including Breakfast alongside the headline titles — offering something for viewers across taste profiles, from action to drama. The volume of content arriving in a single week underscores how significantly OTT platforms have expanded the market for regional-language Indian cinema beyond traditional theatrical footprints.

For South Indian film enthusiasts and casual OTT subscribers alike, the June 22-28 window is one of the more rewarding weeks of the year so far, with enough variety to sustain interest across platforms and languages.
key_facts: Blast, Lingam, and Ananthan Kaadu lead South releases this week
Release window: June 22-28, 2026
At least 9 South OTT titles available this week per Moneycontrol
Releases span theatrical and OTT formats

stories.headline: Ram Kapoor hugs Ekta Kapoor, addresses their public feud: 'It's nobody's business'
stories.what: Actor Ram Kapoor and television mogul Ekta Kapoor have publicly buried what appeared to be a long-standing rift, with Ram embracing Ekta and telling the press that the details of their dispute are 'nobody's business'. The gesture, captured and widely circulated, effectively draws a curtain over what had become a topic of public speculation. Both figures are giants of Indian television, making their apparent reconciliation a significant moment in the industry.
stories.lens: Ram and Ekta Kapoor together shaped a generation of Indian prime-time television, so their public reconciliation closes a chapter that had cast a shadow over both their legacies.
stories.article:
Ram Kapoor and Ekta Kapoor appear to have put their much-speculated feud firmly to rest. At a public event, the veteran actor embraced the producer who helped define his career, and when asked about the falling-out that had been a subject of industry gossip and media conjecture, Ram was pointedly brief: it was, he said, 'nobody's business'.

The two share deep professional history. Ekta Kapoor's Balaji Telefilms was the production house behind several of the shows that made Ram Kapoor a household name in Indian television during the 2000s, a golden era for the melodramatic family dramas that Balaji pioneered. Their reported estrangement had therefore felt like a rupture in something foundational to that chapter of Indian television.

Ram's refusal to elaborate, while simultaneously choosing to make the reconciliation visible, is a carefully calibrated move — acknowledging that the public had noticed the rift while firmly refusing to feed it further. Ekta Kapoor, who has built one of India's most powerful entertainment empires spanning TV, film, and digital, did not publicly comment beyond the gesture itself.

For fans of Indian television's most prolific era, the image of the two together is likely all the closure they were going to get — and Ram Kapoor's pointed 'nobody's business' may well be the most dignified note on which the episode could end.
key_facts: Ram Kapoor publicly hugged Ekta Kapoor, signalling reconciliation
Called details of their feud 'nobody's business'
Both are major figures in Indian television history

stories.headline: Tamannaah exposes disturbing gaze female actors face in South cinema: 'Not complimentary'
stories.what: Actress Tamannaah Bhatia has spoken out about the unsettling male gaze that women in South Indian cinema routinely face, calling it far from flattering or complimentary. In a candid interview reported by The Indian Express, she described the experience as deeply uncomfortable, lending her prominent voice to a growing chorus of women in the industry demanding better treatment on and off screen. Her remarks arrive at a time of heightened scrutiny of gender dynamics in South Indian film industries.
stories.lens: When a mainstream star of Tamannaah's stature names the problem publicly, it puts pressure on a South film industry still processing the fallout of the Hema Committee report's uncomfortable revelations.
stories.article:
Tamannaah Bhatia has broken her customary reticence on industry politics, speaking bluntly about the objectifying male gaze that female actors navigate in South Indian cinema. In an interview with The Indian Express, the actress was unequivocal: the attention women receive from certain quarters of the industry is, as she put it, 'not complimentary'. Her description of the experience as 'disturbing' signals that even commercially successful, well-established women in the industry do not feel insulated from it.

Tamannaah's intervention carries particular weight given her position — she is one of the most bankable actresses across Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi cinema, with decades of top-line billing behind her. For her to publicly call out an entrenched culture of objectification is a departure from the careful image management that most stars of her standing typically maintain.

Her remarks land in a charged moment for South Indian cinema. The Justice Hema Committee report released last year exposed systemic sexual harassment and exploitation in the Malayalam film industry, triggering arrests, public confessions, and industry-wide reckonings. While the Tamil and Telugu industries have faced similar accusations for years, institutional responses have been slower and more muted.

Tamannaah's words are likely to amplify calls for concrete structural reform — from stronger on-set codes of conduct to genuine representation of women in production decision-making roles — in an industry whose global ambitions are increasingly at odds with its internal culture.
key_facts: Tamannaah Bhatia calls male gaze in South cinema 'not complimentary'
Describes the experience as disturbing
Remarks reported by The Indian Express

stories.headline: Tamannaah Bhatia net worth: The Rs 120 crore fortune behind Bollywood and South cinema's biggest names
stories.what: A detailed profile by The Economic Times has put Tamannaah Bhatia's net worth at Rs 120 crore, breaking down the actress's wealth across luxury cars and a substantial real estate portfolio. The figure reflects decades of consistent work across Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi cinema, as well as brand endorsements and digital projects that have made her one of the most financially successful actresses of her generation. The profile arrives alongside her vocal comments on gender dynamics in South cinema.
stories.lens: At Rs 120 crore, Tamannaah's wealth profile shows how a female actor who built her career across multiple South Indian industries — rather than staking everything on Bollywood — can accumulate significant financial independence.
stories.article:
Tamannaah Bhatia's net worth stands at Rs 120 crore, according to a detailed profile published by The Economic Times, encompassing luxury vehicles and a diversified real estate portfolio built across years of sustained success in Hindi, Telugu, and Tamil cinema. The figure makes her one of the wealthiest actresses of her generation in India, and the breakdown of her assets offers a rare, concrete look at how cross-industry appeal translates into financial security in Indian entertainment.

Unlike many contemporaries who have anchored their careers and commercial value primarily to Bollywood, Tamannaah has consistently maintained a strong presence in South Indian films — a strategy that has paid dividends not just in critical goodwill but in sustained earning power. High-profile roles in franchise films, coupled with major brand endorsements and a strong OTT footprint in recent years, have contributed to the Rs 120 crore valuation.

The profile lands on the same day that Tamannaah made headlines for speaking out about the uncomfortable male gaze women face in South Indian cinema — a juxtaposition that is hard to ignore. Her financial independence arguably gives her the platform and security to make such statements without the career anxiety that might silence other actresses at an earlier stage.

For aspirants in the film industry, Tamannaah's profile is a case study in longevity through diversification — across languages, genres, and revenue streams — in an industry where most careers peak early and decline sharply.
key_facts: Tamannaah Bhatia's net worth reported at Rs 120 crore
Assets include luxury cars and real estate
Built across Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi film careers
